text.title: Error Budget Policy and Freeze Rules Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Error Budget Policy and Freeze Rules methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Policy MUST be written, signed by engineering leader + product VP, and stored in version control. Verbal policy is rejected.
rule.rationale: Verbal policy drifts; written policy is the contract that anchors action.

rule.statement: Policy MUST name who is authorised to declare a freeze (e.g. on-call SRE + service owner; engineering leader for portfolio-wide). No ambiguous 'someone senior'.
rule.rationale: Ambiguity stalls action when minutes matter; named authorisation removes hesitation.

rule.statement: During a freeze, allowed: security patches + rollbacks + tier-0 bugfixes. Forbidden: new features + non-critical experiments + capacity changes that aren't safety-related.
rule.rationale: Total freeze blocks even mitigation; allow-list keeps the team able to recover.

rule.statement: Freeze revert criteria MUST be objective (budget remaining >= 20%, burn rate < 1.0 for 24h). Auto-lift when criteria met; no separate manual decision.
rule.rationale: Manual lift becomes political; auto-lift on criteria removes the politics.

rule.statement: Freeze declaration + lift MUST be announced to product / sales / support within 15 min. Comms include reason, allowed work, expected lift criteria.
rule.rationale: Silent freeze produces surprise + escalation from product; comms keep the org aligned.

rule.statement: Policy reviewed yearly with engineering leader + product. Updates capture changes in SLO targets, team structure, customer commitments.
rule.rationale: Stale policy normalises burn; yearly review forces re-alignment.
